name: Circular Route of the Castillo de Nogales and the Sier
name_original: Castillo de Nogales por las Sierras de Tierra de Barros — circular desde Santa Marta de los Barros
description: This difficult 39.5-mile road cycling route takes you through the Sierras de Tierra de Barros to the historic Castillo de Nogales.
region_intro: Road cycling around Santa Marta offers a dynamic environment where the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta meets the Caribbean Sea. The region is characterized by diverse landscapes, including towering mountains, coastal roads, and lush foothills around Minca. Cyclists encounter varied terrains, from challenging ascents through cloud forests to winding routes past coffee and cocoa plantations. This blend provides a range of road cycling experiences within a unique ecological setting. Best road cycling routes around Santa Marta The most popular road cycling route is Circular Route of the Castillo de Nogales and the Sier, a 39.5 miles (63.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Feria Castle – Dehesa Extremeña loop from Nogales, a difficult 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and expansive mountain views. Local road cyclists also love the Views of Feria – Fuente del Maestre loop from Santa Marta de los Barros, a 32.7 miles (52.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Santa Marta is defined by the towering Sierra Nevada, winding coastal paths, and routes through coffee plantations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging multi-hour journeys. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 235 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Marta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Santa Marta?
A: Road cycling around Santa Marta offers a dynamic mix of landscapes. You'll encounter routes where the towering Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta meets the Caribbean Sea, providing varied terrains from challenging ascents through cloud forests to winding roads past coffee and cocoa plantations. The foothills around Minca, in particular, feature cooler climates and spectacular mountain views.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Santa Marta region?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes documented around Santa Marta, catering to various skill levels. These include 8 moderate and 13 difficult routes, ensuring options for both experienced cyclists and those looking for a significant challenge.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, Santa Marta is known for its challenging climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991767188/circular-route-of-the-castillo-de-nogales-and-the-sier" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route of the Castillo de Nogales and the Sier&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Feria Castle – Dehesa Extremeña loop from Nogales&lt;/a&gt;, which offers challenging climbs and expansive mountain views.

Q: What are some scenic natural features I might see while road cycling in Santa Marta?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to cycle through vibrant jungle, past coffee and cocoa plantations, and alongside rivers. The Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta provides a stunning backdrop, and routes in the Minca area offer views of cloud forests and waterfalls like Pozo Azul and Marinka. Coastal routes also provide breathtaking views where the rainforest meets the Caribbean Sea.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes pass by historical and cultural points of interest. For example, you might encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1581105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Feria Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights in the broader area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1124589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Hermitage of La Parra&lt;/a&gt; and ancient natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santa Marta?
A: While Santa Marta offers cycling opportunities year-round, July and October are noted as popular months for cyclists. The region's climate is tropical, so it's advisable to consider the wet and dry seasons. The foothills, like Minca, generally offer cooler climates.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes for those not seeking extreme challenges?
A: Yes, there are several moderate routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31864486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Feria – Fuente del Maestre loop from Santa Marta de los Barros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.7-mile trail leading through varied landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvatierra de los Barros – Castillo de Feria loop from La Morera&lt;/a&gt;, which is also rated as moderate.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Santa Marta?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the towering Sierra Nevada to the Caribbean coast. The blend of challenging mountain ascents, scenic routes through coffee plantations, and the unique ecological setting are often highlighted as key attractions. The variety of routes caters to different abilities, making it appealing to a broad range of riders.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Santa Marta are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991767188/circular-route-of-the-castillo-de-nogales-and-the-sier" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route of the Castillo de Nogales and the Sier&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129259" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Nogales&lt;/a&gt;, offering a convenient return to your starting point.

Q: Can I find routes that combine mountain challenges with coastal views?
A: Absolutely. The unique geography of Santa Marta, where the Sierra Nevada meets the Caribbean, allows for routes that offer both. You can experience challenging ascents through jungle-covered mountains and then descend towards stunning beaches and bays like Taganga or Bahía Concha, providing a diverse cycling experience.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for their coffee or cocoa plantations along cycling routes?
A: Yes, the foothills of the Sierra Nevada, particularly around Minca, are renowned for their coffee and cocoa plantations. Cycling routes in this area wind through these vibrant agricultural landscapes, offering a chance to experience local culture and enjoy cooler climates.
tips: Salvatierra de los Barros is a municipality known internationally for what has been one of its economic pillars for centuries: pottery. Along with the ceramic industry, the breeding of the Iberian pig raised in freedom among holm oaks and cork oaks stands out.

Road that connects the towns of Salvatierra de los Barros with the center of Salvaleón, where it winds along a road where you can see the typical landscape of this Extremadura area, the so-called Dehesas: oak forests with little undergrowth.
The castle and the church that are at the top of the town offer views of the plain called Tierra de Barros.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Santa Marta de los Barros. You'll typically find parking options within the town itself to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult for racebike cycling. It involves a significant elevation gain of over 900 meters and covers more than 63 kilometers, requiring very good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, making it suitable for road bikes. However, expect varied terrain with substantial climbs and descents as you navigate through the Sierras de Tierra de Barros.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through the settlement of Salvatierra de los Barros and enjoy a View of the Sierra de Tierra de Barros. A major highlight is the historic Nogales Castle and Church of San Cristóbal, offering panoramic views.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Considering the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling in Extremadura, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route through the Sierras de Tierra de Barros. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in outdoor areas, this route is designed for racebike cycling on paved roads. It might not be ideal for dogs, especially given the distance and difficulty. If you do bring a dog, ensure it can keep pace safely and is always on a leash.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Salvatierra de los Barros and near Nogales, where you may find local cafes or restaurants. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on a longer, difficult ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several official paths, including sections of the Cañada real, sendero del pilar, Camino de los Majadales, Camino de San Pedro, Camino de la Parra, Camino de la Fuente, Camino de Valdihuelo, Camino de los Hitos, and Camino de Peñagorda.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque countryside views, including agricultural landscapes of the 'Tierra de Barros' (land of clays), and dramatic vistas of the Sierras. The historic Castillo de Nogales provides a stunning focal point.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, pack plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire for the weather, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A GPS device or the komoot app is also recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: How can I best prepare for the challenging climbs on this route?
faqs.answer: This route has significant elevation gain. To best prepare, ensure you have a good level of fitness and consider training on hilly terrain beforehand. Pacing yourself, especially on the ascents, and carrying enough hydration and nutrition will be key to a successful ride.

waypoints.name: View of the Sierra de Tierra de Barros
Nogales Castle and Church of San Cristóbal
